attempts.code_tried:
noncomputable def HModule'_δ
    (k : Type u) [Field k]
    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₀).obj (Opposite.op S.X₁) ⟶
      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₁).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄) :=
  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact k S).extClass.precomp _ (by omega))
attempts.insight: The .extClass.precomp dot-notation chain auto-resolves through CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.precomp without manual Abelian. qualification — confirms the iter-021 plan-agent finding that Mathlib's dot-notation works through the implicit Abelian.Ext type even though the file does not open Abelian. The [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))] typeclass is load-bearing (probe-verified) and matches the typeclass list of iter-016/017/018. Arithmetic side-condition (1 + n₀ = n₁) discharged by `by omega` from the standing `n₀ + 1 = n₁`. AddCommGrpCat.ofHom wraps the resulting AddMonoidHom into a categorical morphism in AddCommGrpCat — needed because the codomain (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (op X) lands in AddCommGrpCat (forced by Mathlib's Abelian.extFunctor n always landing there, regardless of Linear k enrichment). lean_verify returns kernel-only axioms [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]; the L397 'local instance' source-scan warning is the harmless heuristic match on the iter-019 ModuleCat_free_isLeftAdjoint docstring text 'project-local instance'.

next_steps: iter-022+ scaffolds the ComposableArrows-form LES sequence HModule'_sequence (Mathlib L120–122 mirror), consuming S.HModule'_δ k F n₀ n₁ h directly. iter-023+ scaffolds the iso HModule'_sequenceIso to Ext.contravariantSequence (Mathlib L124–138, requiring four substantive auxiliary lemmas L48–106). iter-024+ scaffolds the exactness theorem HModule'_sequence_exact (Mathlib L140–141) plus the δ_toBiprod / fromBiprod_δ simp companions (Mathlib L143–149).
